description: the headless agent's terminal — run one command line, confined, behind an un-liftable destructive floor that self-tests before every run
rules: NEVER run outside the confined sandbox — the floor is defense-in-depth; confinement (no network, dropped privileges, only the cargo workspace bound rw) is the guarantee
the floor is a PURE predicate (hermes_floor.sh) in blessed chip code — the agent supplies var VALUES only and no agent-side permission mode can lift it
step 1 (hermes_floor_check) classifies the pinned case table and refuses the run if the floor misclassifies ANY case — fail closed, before CMD is even looked at
the floor fails toward blocking: quotes are stripped before matching, so a benign command that merely CONTAINS a destructive string (echo "rm -rf /") is refused
WORKDIR must resolve inside the cargo workspace — cwd escapes are refused
usage: Set CMD (the command line). Optional WORKDIR (defaults to the cargo workspace root) and OUT (capture path, defaults to exec.out). Output: floor_report.json (the floor's self-test proof), exec.out (captured stdout+stderr), and a JSON status line per tool.
limitation: The floor is a conservative denylist, not a shell parser — it blocks the classic irrecoverables (recursive rm of absolute/home/workspace paths, mkfs, dd-to-device, fork bombs, writes to system dirs) and accepts false positives to stay simple. A determined bypass is stopped by confinement, not by this table.
